title: How Deploying a Digital Store on GitHub Pages Works and How to Set It Up Today for Digital Creators
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: If you're a digital creator in Nigeria or another restricted country, trying to sell ebooks, courses, or software, but getting blocked by PayPal, Stripe, or other payment processors, you're not alone. You're part of a growing community of creators who've been excluded from the global digital economy due to geographic restrictions, banking limitations, or KYC requirements. In this guide, we'll show you how to deploy a digital store on GitHub Pages and accept crypto payments with ease, using the Full Stack Bundle, a comprehensive store infrastructure that includes Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, all in one payment. By following these steps, you'll be able to reach your global audience and start selling your digital products without any hassle.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

Deploying a digital store on GitHub Pages is a game-changer for creators who've been shut out by traditional payment processors. With GitHub Pages, you can host your store for free, and use the Core Shop Engine to automate delivery and payment verification, without requiring any payment processor accounts. This means you can sell your digital products globally, without worrying about KYC requirements or geographic restrictions. Plus, with Web3 Wallet Connect, you can integrate crypto payments seamlessly, and offer your customers a secure and convenient way to purchase your products.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you begin, you'll need to set up a GitHub Pages account and create a new repository for your store. You'll also need to install the Full Stack Bundle, which includes the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard. This bundle will provide you with a comprehensive store infrastructure that includes automated delivery, payment verification, and transaction logs, all in one payment. Make sure to follow the installation instructions carefully, and test your store thoroughly before going live.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

Once you have the Full Stack Bundle installed, you can set up your payment and delivery pipeline using the Core Shop Engine. This involves configuring your payment settings, setting up automated delivery, and creating PDF receipts for your customers. With Web3 Wallet Connect, you can also integrate crypto payments seamlessly, and offer your customers a secure and convenient way to purchase your products. Make sure to test your payment and delivery pipeline thoroughly before going live.
GitHub Pages is a free and secure hosting solution for digital stores that accept crypto payments.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

With your store set up and payment pipeline in place, you're ready to go live and start selling your digital products. Make sure to test your store thoroughly before launching, and have a plan in place for handling customer support and inquiries. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll also have access to priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide, which will help you scale your store and increase sales. Don't be afraid to promote your store on social media and other channels to get the word out and drive traffic to your store.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

Once your store is live and generating sales, it's time to scale and grow. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide, which will help you stay ahead of the curve and optimize your store for maximum sales. Make sure to monitor your analytics and adjust your marketing strategy accordingly, and don't be afraid to experiment with new ideas and tactics to drive growth and revenue. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have everything you need to scale your store and achieve success as a digital creator.
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

The correct setup sequence matters because each component depends on the previous one being configured correctly. Start with NOWPayments: create your account, add your wallet addresses for each coin you want to accept, and generate your API key. Then deploy the Core Shop Engine: upload your product files, configure your NOWPayments API credentials, set your product prices in USD, and deploy to your hosting environment. Then configure Web3 Wallet Connect: connect it to your Core Shop Engine endpoint, set up your product display, and test the checkout flow in a browser. Finally, complete a real mainnet test transaction with a small amount of actual crypto. Confirm the webhook fires, the engine processes it, the delivery email arrives, and the download link works. Only after that test passes should you start driving traffic to your store.
The Full Stack Bundle includes everything you need to get started, including automated delivery, payment verification, and transaction logs.
